name: LiveAgent
one_liner: Help desk and live chat support software with a REST API and a vendor-built MCP server for ticket, contact, and agent automation.
access_notes: LiveAgent offers a free plan (no credit card) and a 30-day free trial (no credit card). Admins generate API keys under Configuration > System > API for either API v1 or v3, giving working credentials without payment or human approval. Free plan hides historical data after 7 days, but API access itself is self-serve.
api_notes: Two versions: API v1 (~40 operations across Conversations/tickets, Customers, Companies, Customer Groups, Knowledgebase, Files, Reports, Agents, Online Status) and a newer API v3 with filtering and a Swagger descriptor. v3 authenticates via an 'apikey' header; v1 accepts apikey as a query parameter. JSON default, with XML/YAML options. Rate limit 180 req/min per key. Per-key permissions configurable in v3.
mcp_notes: LiveAgent shipped a built-in (vendor) MCP server as part of its May 2026 product update, exposing its API to external AI tools (Claude Desktop, Cursor, claude.ai, FlowHunt) for ticket management, agent/contact operations, and department routing, respecting agent identity and permissions.
main_blocker: None significant; standard REST API with API-key auth and self-serve credentials. Webhook/event support is not documented, so real-time triggers would rely on polling.

evidence.claim: REST API with API v1 and v3, API keys generated under Configuration > System > API; v3 uses 'apikey' header; rate limit 180 req/min per key.
Complete API reference lists resource categories: Conversations, Customers, Companies, Customer Groups, Knowledgebase, Files, Reports, Agents, Online Status.
LiveAgent's built-in MCP server connects external AI tools (Claude Desktop, Cursor, claude.ai, FlowHunt) to ticket data and actions.
LiveAgent offers a free plan (no credit card) and a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

open_questions: Whether the free plan permits API key generation with the same breadth as paid plans; exact operation count and CRUD coverage of API v3 vs v1; whether any webhook/callback mechanism exists beyond polling.
